message:
Close issue bd-3nby: dead_code allow attribute cleanup audit complete

Mark bd-3nby as closed after removing 3 truly dead items (ctw.rs
has_child/child_mut methods, dependency_loss.rs from_impact_components)
and auditing remaining 12 #[allow(dead_code)] attributes as legitimate
(struct fields for deserialization, test-only constructors, documented
placeholders).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3k9b","title":"E2E: bundle encryption + verification flows","description":"## Goal
Validate bundle encryption/decryption and checksum verification end-to-end with detailed logging and failure-path coverage.

## Scope
- `pt bundle create --encrypt` with passphrase arg and env var.
- `pt bundle inspect` + `extract` with verification on/off.
- Failure modes: wrong passphrase, corrupted bundle, missing manifest.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: encrypted bundle, inspect output, extract output, manifest, stderr snapshots.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Encrypted bundles decrypt with correct passphrase only.
- [ ] Verification detects corruption.
- [ ] Failure paths produce clear errors + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted per case.

## Test Plan
- E2E script generates bundle from fixture session and exercises encrypt/decrypt/verify.

 {"id":"bd-3kk2","title":"No-mock inference + ledger regression suite","description":"## Goal
Build a no-mock, deterministic regression suite for inference/posterior math and the evidence ledger.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Capture **realistic evidence fixtures** from live runs (CPU/IO/TTY/orphan/parent/cgroup/net), store as versioned test data.
- End-to-end inference pipeline on fixtures (collection features -> posterior -> confidence -> ledger), **no mocks**.
- Golden outputs for:
  - posterior class scores + confidence labels
  - top evidence factors with contributions and ordering
  - any derived summaries used by agent/TUI outputs
- Property tests:
  - posterior normalization (sum≈1)
  - monotonicity for key signals (e.g., longer idle increases P(abandoned))
  - sensitivity to missing evidence (graceful degradation)
- Numeric stability regression tests (NaN/Inf handling, underflow protection).

## No-mock Policy (Commentary)
- Use recorded fixtures and **actual inference code paths**; no trait mocks or fake implementations.
- Deterministic seeds and fixed fixtures for repeatability.
- Golden output updates require explicit approval (guardrail against drifting expectations).

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs per test case: fixture_id, posterior summary, top factors, confidence labels, runtime.
- Log schema fields: event, timestamp, phase, case_id, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Redact/normalize any sensitive command strings in logs.
- Store artifacts under `test/artifacts/inference/<case>/` for CI upload.

## E2E Coverage
- Add `test/inference_e2e.bats` that runs `pt agent plan` on a fixture session and validates output schema + ledger summary.
- E2E uses runner harness (process_triage-aii.7.7) and emits JSONL logs using the shared schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Inference on fixtures produces stable outputs (golden tests).
- [ ] Posterior outputs normalized and within defined tolerance.
- [ ] Evidence ledger ordering and contributions validated.
- [ ] Failure modes (NaN/Inf/empty evidence) covered deterministically.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted per test case with stable schema.
- [ ] E2E fixture plan run validates output schema + ledger summary.

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden inference cases + property checks.
- Integration: pipeline from fixture -> features -> posterior -> ledger.
- E2E: fixture plan run with JSONL logging.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:11.073333494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:29.232381500Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:29.232311591Z","close_reason":"Implemented inference fixtures + nomock regression suite + E2E test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3lux","title":"No-mock wrapper workflow tests (pt bash wrapper, real processes)","description":"## Goal
Add **no-mock** coverage for the bash `pt` wrapper using real processes and the real `pt-core` binary, with standardized JSONL logging.

## Scope
- Build or download a real `pt-core` binary for test runs (no stubs).
- Spawn short-lived real processes (sleep loops, IO-bound, CPU-bound) and run `pt scan` / `pt agent plan` against them.
- Validate wrapper discovery logic (`PT_CORE_PATH`, PATH search, local `target/release`).
- Exercise wrapper update path in dry-run mode (no network) and ensure safe error messaging when tools missing.
- Verify wrapper respects `--version`, `--help`, and forwards args without mutation.

## No-mock Policy (Commentary)
- Do **not** stub `ps`, `kill`, or `pt-core` in PATH.
- Use real processes + real binaries; rely on harness to clean up safely.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs per case: event, timestamp, phase, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifact bundle: `stdout`, `stderr`, `plan.json`, and harness metadata for each case.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Wrapper works end-to-end against real `pt-core` in CI.
- [ ] Real processes are detected and included in scan/plan output deterministically.
- [ ] Discovery and forwarding logic validated across PT_CORE_PATH/PATH scenarios.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- Integration: spawn real processes; run wrapper commands via harness.
- E2E: `pt scan`, `pt agent plan`, `pt --version`, update error paths.
- Logging: validate JSONL schema + artifact manifest.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:41:23.918040317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T01:29:21.826141118Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:29:21.826070908Z","close_reason":"Aligned wrapper workflow tests to pt-core; added nomock/e2e rust suites + fixtures; BATS + cargo tests green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3lux","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3nxm","title":"Add inline mode support for CLI+TUI coexistence","description":"# Add Inline Mode Support

## What
Leverage ftui's unique inline mode capability to provide a CLI+TUI hybrid experience.
Instead of taking over the full terminal (alt-screen), the TUI renders at the bottom
N rows while scan progress and log output scroll above.

## Why This Matters
Currently, the TUI takes over the full terminal (alt-screen mode). When you quit, all
TUI state disappears. With inline mode:
- Scan progress messages scroll above the TUI
- Log output (warnings, errors) is visible during TUI interaction
- Terminal scrollback preserves history after TUI exits
- Users can scroll up to see scan output while browsing processes

## ftui Inline Mode
```rust
let config = ProgramConfig {
    screen_mode: ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: 20 },
    // ...
};
```

- `ui_height`: Fixed number of rows for the TUI at bottom of terminal
- Above the TUI: normal scrollback region for logs and output
- `Cmd::log("message")` writes to the scrollback region above
- Auto-remeasure: ftui can adjust ui_height based on content

## Implementation
1. Add `--inline` flag to `run --interactive` command
2. Default to alt-screen (current behavior) for backwards compatibility
3. When `--inline`, use `ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: computed }`
4. Compute ui_height based on terminal height (e.g., terminal_height - 5 for scroll area)
5. Scan progress output uses `Cmd::log()` to appear in scroll area
6. Detail pane may need to be more compact in inline mode

## Integration with Progress Emitter
pt-core has a `ProgressEmitter` for scan progress. In inline mode, connect it to
`Cmd::log()` so scan progress appears in the scrollback above the TUI.

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/main.rs` — add --inline flag, configure ScreenMode
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/app.rs` — handle inline-specific layout constraints

## Acceptance Criteria
- `--inline` flag enables inline mode
- Default (no flag) uses alt-screen (unchanged behavior)
- Scan progress scrolls above TUI in inline mode
- TUI renders at bottom of terminal
- Terminal scrollback preserved after exit
- Layout adapts to inline constraints","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:15:53.915574064Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:21:16.147201238Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:21:16.147123153Z","close_reason":"Implemented --inline TUI mode; Cmd::log progress; docs + test gating fixes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["enhancement","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nxm","depends_on_id":"bd-3ip2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3nxm","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3p52","title":"Port ProcessDetail widget (3 sub-views) to ftui","description":"# Port ProcessDetail Widget to ftui (with Evidence Ledger)

## What
Migrate the ProcessDetail widget (tui/widgets/process_detail.rs, 716 lines) which renders
a multi-view detail pane with 3 sub-views: Summary, Genealogy, and Galaxy Brain.

This bead also incorporates the evidence ledger display from the Phase 7 UX requirements
(process_triage-2ka). The evidence ledger provides an exportable, scrollable audit trail
of all Bayesian evidence contributions — crucial for the "explain decisions" goal.

---

## Current ProcessDetail Structure

### DetailView enum
```rust
pub enum DetailView {
    Summary,     // Process info, classification, confidence, evidence
    Genealogy,   // Process family tree (parent/child relationships)
    GalaxyBrain, // Full mathematical analysis (posterior distributions)
}
```

### Summary View Content
- Process classification (KILL/REVIEW/SPARE) with colored badge
- Confidence level (e.g., "High (0.92)")
- Why summary (1-2 sentence explanation)
- Top evidence lines (bullet list)
- Execution plan preview (if selected)

### Genealogy View Content
- Parent process info
- Child process list
- Process tree visualization

### Galaxy Brain View Content
- Full posterior distribution breakdown
- Log odds for each classification
- Evidence contribution per feature
- Mathematical trace of Bayesian inference
- Rendered as scrollable styled text with color-coded numbers

---

## ftui Widget Mapping

### For Summary View
- Use ftui `Block` with title for the container
- Use ftui `Paragraph` for text sections
- Use ftui `Badge` for classification label (KILL/REVIEW/SPARE)
- Use ftui `List` for evidence bullet points
- Vertical `Flex` layout to stack sections

### For Genealogy View
- Use ftui `Tree` widget for hierarchical process tree
- This is a major upgrade — current code draws the tree manually with ASCII art
- ftui Tree supports expand/collapse, lazy loading, custom item rendering

### For Galaxy Brain View
- Use ftui `Paragraph` with rich styled text (Spans/Lines)
- Use ftui `Scrollbar` for scrolling through long analysis
- Color-coded numbers: positive log-odds green, negative red
- Could potentially use ftui `JsonView` for structured data display

---

## Evidence Ledger (from Phase 7 UX requirements)

The Galaxy Brain sub-view should include or link to an evidence ledger that provides:
- Complete audit trail of Bayesian evidence contributions
- Each evidence item: feature name, observed value, log Bayes factor, direction
- Sortable by impact (absolute log Bayes factor)
- Exportable to file (`e` key in Galaxy Brain view → save to JSONL)

### Evidence Ledger Display
```
Evidence Ledger for PID 12345 (rsyslogd)
═══════════════════════════════════════
 #  Feature         Observed  logBF   Direction
 1  cpu_fraction    0.02      +3.21   → spare
 2  runtime_sec     86400     +2.44   → useful
 3  has_tty         false     -1.87   → abandoned
 4  orphan          false     +1.52   → useful
 5  net_active      true      +0.98   → useful
 6  io_active       false     -0.67   → abandoned
 7  command_cat     daemon    +0.45   → useful
 8  state_flag      Sleeping  +0.12   → useful
───────────────────────────────────────
    Net logBF: +6.18  →  Classification: SPARE (p=0.94)
```

### Export to File
When user presses `x` (export) in Galaxy Brain view:
```rust
Msg::ExportEvidenceLedger => {
    let pid = self.selected_process_pid();
    let ledger = self.build_evidence_ledger(pid);
    Cmd::perform(
        async move { save_ledger_to_file(ledger).await },
        Msg::LedgerExported,
    )
}
```

---

## Tab Bar for Sub-Views
Use ftui's `Tabs` widget to show [Summary | Genealogy | Galaxy Brain] tabs at the top
of the detail pane. This provides clear visual affordance for view switching:
```rust
let tabs = Tabs::new(vec\!["Summary", "Genealogy", "Galaxy Brain"])
    .select(self.active_detail_view as usize)
    .style(sheet.get("tabs.inactive"))
    .highlight_style(sheet.get("tabs.active"));
frame.render_widget(tabs, tabs_area);
```
Current code just switches content without tab indicators — this is a UX improvement.

---

## View Switching
In ftui Elm architecture, `DetailView` is part of Model state. The `s`/`t`/`g` keys
produce `Msg::SetDetailView(DetailView::Summary)` etc. The `view()` function checks
which sub-view to render and dispatches accordingly.

---

## Unit Tests (~8)
```rust
#[test]
fn summary_view_shows_classification_badge() { ... }
#[test]
fn genealogy_tree_shows_parent_and_children() { ... }
#[test]
fn galaxy_brain_renders_log_odds_with_colors() { ... }
#[test]
fn evidence_ledger_sorts_by_impact() { ... }
#[test]
fn tab_indicator_shows_active_view() { ... }
#[test]
fn view_switch_updates_content() { ... }
#[test]
fn detail_updates_on_selection_change() { ... }
#[test]
fn scrolling_works_in_galaxy_brain() { ... }
```

---

## Files to Modify
- `crates/pt-core/src/tui/widgets/process_detail.rs` — rewrite using ftui widgets
- Consider splitting into sub-modules if the file gets large

## Acceptance Criteria
- Summary view shows classification badge, confidence, evidence, plan preview
- Genealogy view uses ftui Tree for process hierarchy (expand/collapse)
- Galaxy Brain view renders scrollable mathematical analysis with colored numbers
- Evidence ledger display with sortable impact column
- Export evidence ledger to JSONL file via `x` key
- Tab indicator shows active sub-view ([Summary | Genealogy | Galaxy Brain])
- View switches via s/t/g keys
- Content updates when selected process changes
- Scrolling works within detail pane (for long Galaxy Brain + ledger output)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:10:10.949503718Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T01:37:18.296578992Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:37:18.296498553Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tui","widget"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p52","depends_on_id":"bd-2hmx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3p52","depends_on_id":"bd-3hpi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3p52","depends_on_id":"bd-3qvi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3p52","depends_on_id":"bd-3w07","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:02Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3p7w","title":"E2E coverage remaining surfaces (shadow/diff/telemetry/config/bundle-enc)","description":"## Goal
Fill remaining end-to-end integration coverage gaps with rich JSONL logging + artifacts.

## Scope (Child Tasks)
- Shadow mode lifecycle (start/run/stop/export/report).
- Session diff/compare workflows (pt diff + agent diff).
- Telemetry maintenance (status/prune/retention).
- Config preset workflows (list/show/diff/export/validate).
- Bundle encryption/verification flows (passphrase env + CLI).
- Output formatting modes (fields/compact/TOON/truncation).
- Capabilities manifest + cache flows (agent capabilities).
- Supervision gating + protected patterns (plan/action guardrails).
- Policy guardrails (rate limits, blast radius, max kills).
- Degraded environment + permissions (missing deps, permission errors, unsupported features).

## Logging & Artifacts
- All E2E scripts emit JSONL logs with shared schema + artifact manifests.
- Failure-mode coverage required (invalid inputs, missing files, wrong passphrase, malformed policy).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each listed surface has deterministic E2E coverage in CI.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate for every script.
- [ ] Negative-paths are explicitly validated for each surface.
